react_best_practices.title: React & Frontend Best Practices
react_best_practices.description: Guidelines for functional components, hooks, lazy loading, and composition
react_best_practices.content:
# React & Frontend Best Practices

- Component-based architecture with reusable UI units.
- Prefer functional components with hooks.
- Keep components small and feature-oriented.
- Avoid unnecessary re-renders using memoization.
- Use composition over inheritance.
- Use React.lazy + Suspense for all routes and heavy components to reduce initial bundle size.
- Optimize images by using WebP or AVIF formats, and define width/height to prevent layout shifts.
- Avoid importing entire libraries; only import what is needed to ensure proper tree-shaking.

state_management.title: State Management (Zustand & Redux)
state_management.description: Rules for Zustand and Redux stores, actions, and selectors
state_management.content:
# State Management (Zustand & Redux)

## Zustand
- Use for simple, fast, and minimal global state needs.
- Keep stores small and focused on a single domain or feature.
- Prefer it for UI state, client-side interactions, and local app logic.
- Avoid using it as a replacement for server-state management tools.
- Leverage selectors to derive computed state instead of duplicating state.
- Keep TypeScript strict to maintain predictable state shape.

## Redux
- Use when the application requires strict structure and predictable state flow.
- Prefer for large-scale applications with complex shared state.
- Organize logic with slices, reducers, and actions using Redux Toolkit.
- Centralize state changes to ensure traceability and debugging clarity.
- Use DevTools actively to inspect state changes and flows.
- Avoid overusing Redux for simple or local UI state.

data_layer.title: Data Layer (Zod & TanStack Query)
data_layer.description: Caching, mutations, and boundaries validation rules
data_layer.content:
# Data Layer (Zod & TanStack Query)

## TanStack Query
- Use for all server-state (API data), never for UI state.
- Treat the cache as the source of truth for remote data.
- Let it handle caching, retries, refetching, and loading states by default.
- Design query keys carefully and keep them stable and predictable.
- Use mutations for all write operations (create/update/delete).
- Do not duplicate server-state in local state unless strictly necessary.

## Zod
- Define schemas first, then infer TypeScript types from them.
- Use it as the single source of truth for validation logic.
- Validate all external inputs (API, forms, user data) at the boundary.
- Fail fast on invalid data instead of silently coercing state.
- Keep schemas modular and reusable across frontend and backend.

ui_development.title: UI Development (Tailwind CSS, TanStack Table, Sass)
ui_development.description: Styling, headless tables, and consistent design guidelines
ui_development.content:
# UI Development (Tailwind CSS, TanStack Table, Sass)

## Tailwind CSS
- Use utility classes for rapid development and styling consistency.
- Avoid custom CSS files unless utility classes are insufficient.

## Sass
- Use Sass for preprocessed styling (variables, mixins, nesting).

## TanStack Table
- Use as a headless engine: you fully control the UI layer.
- Keep logic (sorting, filtering, pagination) separate from rendering.
- Design tables as composable building blocks, not monolithic components.
- Optimize for large datasets with memoization and virtualization when needed.
- Treat table state as external state when building complex dashboards.

architecture_performance.title: Architecture & Performance
architecture_performance.description: General performance, API optimization, and clean architecture standards
architecture_performance.content:
# Architecture & Performance

- Memoization to avoid unnecessary renders.
- Optimize API calls with caching.
- Use lightweight dependencies.
- Prefer modular architecture.

security.title: Security Guidelines (OWASP)
security.description: Input validation, secure communication, and sanitization standards
security.content:
# Security Guidelines (OWASP)

- Validate all inputs.
- Prevent XSS and injection attacks.
- Secure authentication flows.
- Never use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` without explicit sanitization.
- Never log tokens, passwords, or PII. No sensitive data in unencrypted `localStorage`.
- Use HTTPS in production.
